basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Mujādala — “The Pleading Woman” — takes its name from the woman who pleaded with the Prophet about her husband and carried her complaint to God: “God has heard the words of her who disputes with you…” Qushayrī opens with a basmala on the sincerity of complaint: when she was truthful in her complaint to God and despaired of any other than Him, God sent down concerning her: “God has heard…” — and he draws from it that none makes a loss with God. The sūra regulates the ẓihār (the repudiation by the formula “be to me as my mother's back”), denounces the secret conclaves (najwā) where one incites to sin, and reveals the nearness of the Witness: “there are not three in private converse but He is the fourth.” Qushayrī sets there a decisive pearl: what a difference between him whose fourth is his dog and him whose fourth is his Lord! It ends on the “party of God” (ḥizb Allāh) — those in whose hearts the Name of God is engraved, strengthened by a Spirit from Him.

versets.ar: قَدْ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجَادِلُكَ فِي زَوْجِهَا وَتَشْتَكِي إِلَى اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحَاوُرَكُمَا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: qad samiʿa llāhu qawla llatī tujādiluka fī zawjihā wa-tashtakī ilā llāhi wa-llāhu yasmaʿu taḥāwurakumā inna llāha samīʿun baṣīrun
versets.trad: God has heard the words of her who disputes with you about her husband and complains to God. And God hears your dialogue. Truly, God is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when she was sincere in her complaint to God and despaired of having her wrong lifted by any other than Him, God sent down concerning her: “God has heard…”. She implored God, raised her case to God, spread her anguish before God — then God looked upon her and said: “God has heard.” And he draws the great law: let men know that none makes a loss with God (lā yakhsaru ʿalā llāhi aḥad). The complaint of a single forsaken woman rises to the Throne.

mots.ar: قَدْ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجَادِلُكَ
mots.tr: qad samiʿa llāhu qawla llatī tujādiluka
mots.racine: س م ع / ج د ل
mots.sens: God has heard the words of her who disputes with you
mots.def: jādala: to dispute, plead; samiʿa: to hear.

mots.racine: ش ك و
mots.sens: and complains to God

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.sens: God hears and sees all

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُظَاهِرُونَ مِنكُم مِّن نِّسَائِهِم مَّا هُنَّ أُمَّهَاتِهِمْ ۖ إِنْ أُمَّهَاتُهُمْ إِلَّا اللَّائِي وَلَدْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَقُولُونَ مُنكَرًا مِّنَ الْقَوْلِ وَزُورًا ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ
versets.tr: alladhīna yuẓāhirūna minkum min nisāʾihim mā hunna ummahātihim in ummahātuhum illā llāʾī waladnahum wa-innahum la-yaqūlūna munkaran mina l-qawli wa-zūran wa-inna llāha la-ʿafuwwun ghafūrun
versets.trad: Those of you who repudiate their wives by ẓihār — they are not their mothers; their mothers are only those who bore them. They utter a detestable saying and a falsehood. Yet God is Pardoning, Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: The ẓihār — the pagan formula “be to me as my mother's back” — is a detestable saying and a falsehood (munkar wa-zūr). Qushayrī: this saying God did not validate, and it is not true in itself; no law rests on it — it is pure falsehood and pure lie. And he draws: realities are not strengthened by disguise; an invalid cause does not become valid by repetition. The hollow word has no power, however solemn.

mots.racine: ظ ه ر / ن س و
mots.sens: those who practise ẓihār toward their wives
mots.def: ẓāhara: to repudiate by the back-formula.

mots.racine: أ م م
mots.sens: they are not their mothers

mots.racine: ن ك ر / ز و ر
mots.sens: a detestable saying and a falsehood

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُظَاهِرُونَ مِن نِّسَائِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا قَالُوا فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَاسَّا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yuẓāhirūna min nisāʾihim thumma yaʿūdūna limā qālū fa-taḥrīru raqabatin min qabli an yatamāssā dhālikum tūʿaẓūna bihi wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: Those who repudiate their wives by ẓihār, then wish to retract what they said, must free a slave before they touch one another. By this you are admonished. And God is aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the ẓihār, though it has no basis in reality, once brought before the Prophet did not remain without a ruling: God fixed for it a judgment (freeing a slave). And he draws a consolation: the raising of the matter to the Prophet laid upon the offender the weight of his fault, restored to the woman her right, and settled the question — thus every difficulty is finally resolved, and every night, however long, gives onto the dawn.

mots.racine: ع و د
mots.sens: then wish to retract what they said

mots.ar: فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَاسَّا
mots.tr: fa-taḥrīru raqabatin min qabli an yatamāssā
mots.racine: ح ر ر / ر ق ب / م س س
mots.sens: free a slave before they touch

mots.racine: و ع ظ
mots.sens: by this you are admonished

versets.ar: فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَاسَّا ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَإِطْعَامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِينًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۚ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ ۗ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-man lam yajid fa-ṣiyāmu shahrayni mutatābiʿayni min qabli an yatamāssā fa-man lam yastaṭiʿ fa-iṭʿāmu sittīna miskīnan dhālika li-tuʾminū billāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-tilka ḥudūdu llāhi wa-lil-kāfirīna ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Whoever cannot, let him fast two consecutive months before they touch one another; whoever is unable, let him feed sixty needy persons. This is so that you may believe in God and His Messenger. Such are the limits of God. For the disbelievers there is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Atonement grades by means: to free, else to fast two months, else to feed sixty poor. The verse states the purpose: “so that you may believe in God and His Messenger.” Reparation is no mere tariff but a school of faith: to return to God's right strengthens the heart. “Such are the limits of God” (ḥudūd Allāh) — to stand at the limit is the very heart of servitude.

mots.racine: ص و م / ش ه ر / ت ب ع
mots.sens: fast two consecutive months

mots.racine: ط ع م / س ك ن
mots.sens: feed sixty needy persons

mots.racine: ح د د
mots.sens: such are the limits of God

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ كُبِتُوا كَمَا كُبِتَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ وَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَا آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ ۚ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yuḥāddūna llāha wa-rasūlahu kubitū kamā kubita lladhīna min qablihim wa-qad anzalnā āyātin bayyinātin wa-lil-kāfirīna ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: Those who oppose God and His Messenger will be abased as were those before them. We have already sent down clear signs, and for the disbelievers there is a humiliating punishment.
versets.commentaire: Those who oppose (yuḥāddūn — lit. set themselves at the contrary limit) God and His Messenger are abased (kubitū) like those before them. Qushayrī: God set His custom running: to take vengeance on the criminals; whoever ruins a sunna of the Prophet and brings an innovation into His religion threads himself onto that same string and falls into that abasement. Opposition to the divine right leads to the same humiliation across the ages.

mots.racine: ح د د
mots.sens: those who oppose God and His Messenger
mots.def: ḥādda: to oppose, set at the adverse limit.

mots.ar: كُبِتُوا كَمَا كُبِتَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ
mots.tr: kubitū kamā kubita lladhīna min qablihim
mots.racine: ك ب ت
mots.sens: abased as their predecessors were

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ه و ن
mots.sens: for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا ۚ أَحْصَاهُ اللَّهُ وَنَسُوهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: yawma yabʿathuhumu llāhu jamīʿan fa-yunabbiʾuhum bimā ʿamilū aḥṣāhu llāhu wa-nasūhu wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdun
versets.trad: On the Day God resurrects them all and informs them of what they did. God has counted it while they have forgotten it. God is Witness over all things.
versets.commentaire: God has counted (aḥṣā) what they have forgotten. Qushayrī: when one is brought to account on the Day of Resurrection, the deed re-presents itself to the sinner, he remembers it, as if he stood at that very instant on the carpet of the fault; and he feels such shame and remorse that beside them every torment fades. The worst punishment is this revived memory before the Witness: what man erases from his memory, God keeps inscribed.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: He informs them of what they did

mots.racine: ح ص ي / ن س ي
mots.sens: God counted it, they forgot it
mots.def: aḥṣā: to enumerate; nasiya: to forget.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.sens: God is Witness over all things

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ مَا يَكُونُ مِن نَّجْوَىٰ ثَلَاثَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ رَابِعُهُمْ وَلَا خَمْسَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ سَادِسُهُمْ وَلَا أَدْنَىٰ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَا أَكْثَرَ إِلَّا هُوَ مَعَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كَانُوا ۖ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi mā yakūnu min najwā thalāthatin illā huwa rābiʿuhum wa-lā khamsatin illā huwa sādisuhum wa-lā adnā min dhālika wa-lā akthara illā huwa maʿahum ayna mā kānū thumma yunabbiʾuhum bimā ʿamilū yawma l-qiyāmati inna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Do you not see that God knows what is in the heavens and the earth? There is no private converse of three but He is the fourth, nor of five but He is the sixth, nor of fewer than that nor more but He is with them wherever they are; then He will inform them of what they did on the Day of Resurrection. Truly, God is Knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a summit: the presence of the Real, though universal by knowledge and particular by grace and aid, leaves in the hearts of the gnostics an immense effect: rapture, ecstasy, the delicious daze in the hearing of this word. And he opposes, magnificently: what a difference between him whose fourth is his dog (the People of the Cave) and him whose fourth is his Lord! Then: wherever you are, I am with you: in the mosque, with you; in the tavern, with you.

mots.ar: مَا يَكُونُ مِن نَّجْوَىٰ ثَلَاثَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ رَابِعُهُمْ
mots.tr: mā yakūnu min najwā thalāthatin illā huwa rābiʿuhum
mots.racine: ن ج و / ر ب ع
mots.sens: no converse of three but He is the fourth

mots.ar: إِلَّا هُوَ مَعَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كَانُوا
mots.tr: illā huwa maʿahum ayna mā kānū
mots.racine: ك و ن
mots.sens: but He is with them wherever they are

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.sens: God knows all things

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ نُهُوا عَنِ النَّجْوَىٰ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَيَتَنَاجَوْنَ بِالْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ وَإِذَا جَاءُوكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ وَيَقُولُونَ فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ لَوْلَا يُعَذِّبُنَا اللَّهُ بِمَا نَقُولُ ۚ حَسْبُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ يَصْلَوْنَهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna nuhū ʿani n-najwā thumma yaʿūdūna limā nuhū ʿanhu wa-yatanājawna bi-l-ithmi wa-l-ʿudwāni wa-maʿṣiyati r-rasūli wa-idhā jāʾūka ḥayyawka bimā lam yuḥayyika bihi llāhu wa-yaqūlūna fī anfusihim lawlā yuʿadhdhibunā llāhu bimā naqūlu ḥasbuhum jahannamu yaṣlawnahā fa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: Have you not seen those forbidden private converse who then return to what was forbidden them, conferring in sin, hostility, and disobedience to the Messenger? When they come to you, they greet you with words God did not use to greet you, and they say within themselves: “Why does God not punish us for what we say?” Hell will suffice them; they will burn in it; what a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they wounded the hearts of the Muslims by what they whispered among themselves; their conclave had no aim but to trouble the believers' hearts. The twisted greeting — “they greet you otherwise than God greets you” — targets the hypocrites who slipped a curse under the guise of a salutation. Their secret arrogance — “why does God not punish us?” — is the height of blindness: they defy the very respite that ruins them.

mots.ar: الَّذِينَ نُهُوا عَنِ النَّجْوَىٰ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ
mots.tr: alladhīna nuhū ʿani n-najwā thumma yaʿūdūn
mots.racine: ن ه ي / ن ج و / ع و د
mots.sens: those forbidden conclave who then return to it
mots.def: nuhiya: to be forbidden; najwā: conclave.

mots.racine: ن ج و / أ ث م / ع د و
mots.sens: they confer in sin and hostility
mots.def: tanājā: to confer secretly; ithm: sin; ʿudwān: hostility.

mots.ar: حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ
mots.tr: ḥayyawka bimā lam yuḥayyika bihi llāh
mots.racine: ح ي ي
mots.sens: they greet you otherwise than God greets you

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا تَنَاجَيْتُمْ فَلَا تَتَنَاجَوْا بِالْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَمَعْصِيَتِ الرَّسُولِ وَتَنَاجَوْا بِالْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā tanājaytum fa-lā tatanājaw bi-l-ithmi wa-l-ʿudwāni wa-maʿṣiyati r-rasūli wa-tanājaw bi-l-birri wa-t-taqwā wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī ilayhi tuḥsharūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you confer privately, do not confer in sin, hostility, and disobedience to the Messenger! Confer in goodness and godfearing. And fear God, to whom you will be gathered!
versets.commentaire: To the converse of sin God opposes the converse of goodness (birr) and godfearing (taqwā). Qushayrī: what made the other ugly and grave is that it bore corruption of the bond between people; and the best of deeds is that which leads to the reconciliation of the bond (iṣlāḥ dhāt al-bayn). Secret speech is not evil in itself: all depends on what it sows — discord or concord.

mots.racine: ن ج و / أ ث م
mots.sens: when you confer, not in sin

mots.racine: ب ر ر / و ق ي
mots.sens: confer in goodness and godfearing

mots.racine: و ق ي / ح ش ر
mots.sens: fear God to whom you will be gathered

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا النَّجْوَىٰ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ لِيَحْزُنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَيْسَ بِضَارِّهِمْ شَيْئًا إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: innamā n-najwā mina sh-shayṭāni li-yaḥzuna lladhīna āmanū wa-laysa bi-ḍārrihim shayʾan illā bi-idhni llāhi wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: Private converse is only from Satan, to grieve the believers. Yet it cannot harm them at all save by God's leave. In God let the believers place their trust!
versets.commentaire: The harmful conclave comes from the devil's embellishment, to grieve the believers. Qushayrī: when contemplation prevails, when hearts are present, tawakkul sound and the gaze in its right place — such things have no effect; they reach only the weak. The devil can harm only by God's leave: hence the remedy is tawakkul — to entrust oneself to the One who holds all harm in His hands.

mots.racine: ن ج و / ش ط ن
mots.sens: conclave is only from Satan

mots.ar: وَلَيْسَ بِضَارِّهِمْ شَيْئًا إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ
mots.tr: wa-laysa bi-ḍārrihim shayʾan illā bi-idhni llāh
mots.racine: ض ر ر / أ ذ ن
mots.sens: it harms them only by God's leave

mots.racine: و ك ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: let the believers trust in God

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجَالِسِ فَافْسَحُوا يَفْسَحِ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِذَا قِيلَ انشُزُوا فَانشُزُوا يَرْفَعِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجَاتٍ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā qīla lakum tafassaḥū fī l-majālisi fa-fsaḥū yafsaḥi llāhu lakum wa-idhā qīla nshuzū fa-nshuzū yarfaʿi llāhu lladhīna āmanū minkum wa-lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma darajātin wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you are told, “Make room in the assemblies,” make room — God will make room for you! And when you are told, “Rise up,” rise up — God will raise those of you who believe and those given knowledge in degrees. And God is aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by the fullness of His mercy and the perfection of His tenderness, He teaches them the keeping of fair courtesy among themselves, even in the matters of common usage — making room in assemblies, order in the press. And he marvels: how noble are these people to whom He prescribes the niceties of things, after they have fulfilled the foundations of religion! Whoever makes room, God makes room for him; and He raises the believers and the learned in degrees.

mots.racine: ف س ح / ج ل س
mots.sens: make room in the assemblies
mots.def: tafassaḥa: to make space; majālis: assemblies.

mots.racine: ن ش ز
mots.sens: when told: rise up, do so

mots.ar: يَرْفَعِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجَاتٍ
mots.tr: yarfaʿi llāhu lladhīna āmanū minkum wa-lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma darajāt
mots.racine: ر ف ع / ع ل م / د ر ج
mots.sens: God raises the believers and the learned in degrees
mots.def: rafaʿa: to raise; ʿilm: knowledge; darajāt: degrees.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا نَاجَيْتُمُ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَةً ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ تَجِدُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā nājaytumu r-rasūla fa-qaddimū bayna yaday najwākum ṣadaqatan dhālika khayrun lakum wa-aṭharu fa-in lam tajidū fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you confer privately with the Messenger, give a charity before your private converse. That is better for you and purer. But if you find nothing, God is Forgiving, Compassionate!
versets.commentaire: When private converse with the Prophet was conditioned on a charity, Qushayrī observes: many abstained and renounced — and thus were revealed the jewels of characters and the purity of men. The test, even slight, separates the sincere from the calculating: he who truly loves the Prophet gives without hesitation; he who clings only to his wealth draws back. He cites: “He does not ask you for your wealth; were He to ask and press you, you would be miserly.”

mots.racine: ن ج و
mots.sens: when you confer privately with the Messenger

mots.racine: ق د م / ن ج و / ص د ق
mots.sens: give a charity before your converse
mots.def: qaddama: to put forward; ṣadaqa: charity.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ط ه ر
mots.sens: that is better and purer for you

versets.ar: أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَن تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَتَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: a-ashfaqtum an tuqaddimū bayna yaday najwākum ṣadaqātin fa-idh lam tafʿalū wa-tāba llāhu ʿalaykum fa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-llāhu khabīrun bimā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Are you afraid to give charity before your private converse? Since you did not do so and God turned to you in mercy, then establish the prayer, give the alms, and obey God and His Messenger. And God is aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that this prescription was a brief test, then abrogated: the charity preceding the converse was later permitted without condition; some say it lasted ten nights, then was lifted; some say it lasted only an hour of the day. He reports that ʿAlī alone complied, giving a dirham at each converse, before the abrogation. The rule revealed hearts, then God eased its weight and brought back to essentials: prayer, alms, obedience.

mots.racine: ش ف ق / ق د م
mots.sens: are you afraid to give beforehand

mots.racine: ت و ب
mots.sens: God turned to you in mercy

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و / ز ك و
mots.sens: establish the prayer and give the alms

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا قَوْمًا غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مَّا هُم مِّنكُمْ وَلَا مِنْهُمْ وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna tawallaw qawman ghaḍiba llāhu ʿalayhim mā hum minkum wa-lā minhum wa-yaḥlifūna ʿalā l-kadhibi wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who take as allies a people upon whom God is angry? They are neither of you nor of them, and they swear to falsehood while they know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever sides with one upon whom [divine] wrath weighs shares in deserving the wrath of the Wrathful; whoever takes as ally an object of God's anger draws upon himself God's anger — and that is enough as humiliation and loss. The lying oath, sworn knowingly, is the mark of the hypocrite: he lies to God while knowing he lies.

mots.racine: و ل ي / غ ض ب
mots.sens: they ally with a people God is angry at
mots.def: tawallā: to ally; ghaḍiba: to be angry.

mots.sens: they are neither of you nor of them

mots.racine: ح ل ف / ك ذ ب / ع ل م
mots.sens: they swear to falsehood knowingly

versets.ar: أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ إِنَّهُمْ سَاءَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: aʿadda llāhu lahum ʿadhāban shadīdan innahum sāʾa mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: God has prepared for them a severe punishment. Evil indeed is what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: God has prepared (aʿadda) a severe punishment, a direct consequence of their detestable deeds. The verse links the penalty to the work: it is no arbitrary sanction but the ripening of what they did. “Evil is what they used to do” — their evil bears its own verdict within itself.

mots.racine: ع د د / ع ذ ب / ش د د
mots.sens: God has prepared a severe punishment

mots.racine: س و أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: evil is what they did

versets.ar: اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: ittakhadhū aymānahum junnatan fa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi fa-lahum ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: They take their oaths as a shield and turn people from the way of God. For them is a humiliating punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they take their oaths as a shield and veil (junna); but whoever covers himself with the screen of his obedience to save his world, the arrows of the decree uncover him from where he does not expect — neither his religion remains nor his world is safe. The mask of ostensible devotion protects from nothing: it ends by falling, leaving the hypocrite bare before the punishment.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ي م ن / ج ن ن
mots.sens: they take their oaths as a shield

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: they turn from the way of God

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ه و ن
mots.sens: for them a humiliating punishment

versets.ar: لَّن تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: lan tughniya ʿanhum amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum mina llāhi shayʾan ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūna
versets.trad: Neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against God. Those are the inhabitants of the Fire; they will abide therein forever.
versets.commentaire: Neither wealth nor children save against God. Qushayrī said elsewhere: whoever shelters behind the screen of his obedience to preserve his world ends by losing all. The hoarded wealth and the offspring on which the hypocrite counted for his support weigh nothing on the Day of reckoning: only the truthful heart abides.

mots.ar: لَّن تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُم
mots.tr: lan tughniya ʿanhum amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum
mots.racine: غ ن ي / م و ل / و ل د
mots.sens: their wealth and children will not avail them
mots.def: aghnā: to avail; amwāl: wealth; awlād: children.

mots.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
mots.tr: ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر / خ ل د
mots.sens: they are the inhabitants of the Fire, abiding forever
mots.def: aṣḥāb al-nār: inhabitants of the Fire; khālid: eternal.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَمَا يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ ۚ أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: yawma yabʿathuhumu llāhu jamīʿan fa-yaḥlifūna lahu kamā yaḥlifūna lakum wa-yaḥsabūna annahum ʿalā shayʾin alā innahum humu l-kādhibūna
versets.trad: On the Day God resurrects them all, they will swear to Him as they swear to you, thinking they stand on something. Indeed, it is they who are the liars!
versets.commentaire: The height of blindness: they will swear to God as they swore to men, believing the ruse that worked with creatures will work with the Real. Qushayrī: their greatest punishment is to think that what they did with men will also pass in dealing with the Real; the excess of estrangement and the height of ignorance have cast them on their faces into the chasm of their remorse.

mots.racine: ح ل ف
mots.sens: they swear to Him as they swear to you

mots.racine: ح س ب
mots.sens: they think they stand on something

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
mots.sens: it is they who are the liars

versets.ar: اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الشَّيْطَانُ فَأَنسَاهُمْ ذِكْرَ اللَّهِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطَانِ ۚ أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ الشَّيْطَانِ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: istaḥwadha ʿalayhimu sh-shayṭānu fa-ansāhum dhikra llāhi ulāʾika ḥizbu sh-shayṭāni alā inna ḥizba sh-shayṭāni humu l-khāsirūna
versets.trad: Satan has gained mastery over them and made them forget the remembrance of God. They are the party of Satan. Indeed, the party of Satan are the losers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the devil gains the upper hand over a servant, he makes him forget the remembrance of God; and the soul, when it dominates a man, makes him forget God. He draws a warning: the party of Satan has lost — but more lost still is he who aids his soul, his most relentless enemy, instead of striving to subdue it to escape its evil.

mots.racine: ح و ذ / ش ط ن
mots.sens: Satan gained mastery over them
mots.def: istaḥwadha: to gain the upper hand, dominate.

mots.racine: ن س ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: he made them forget the remembrance of God
mots.def: ansā: to make forget; dhikr: remembrance.

mots.racine: ح ز ب / ش ط ن
mots.sens: they are the party of Satan

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ أُولَٰئِكَ فِي الْأَذَلِّينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yuḥāddūna llāha wa-rasūlahu ulāʾika fī l-adhallīna
versets.trad: Those who oppose God and His Messenger — they are among the most abased.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the law into three sentences: he whom his wretchedness has cast down, his strength does not raise him; he whom the decree has broken, his contriving does not preserve him; he who scorns religion threads himself into the rank of the most abased (al-adhallīn). To oppose God is to condemn oneself to baseness: no human stratagem holds against the divine decree.

mots.racine: ح د د
mots.sens: those who oppose God and His Messenger

mots.racine: ذ ل ل
mots.sens: they are among the most abased
mots.def: adhall: the most base, most humbled.

versets.ar: كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ
versets.tr: kataba llāhu la-aghlibanna anā wa-rusulī inna llāha qawiyyun ʿazīzun
versets.trad: God has decreed: “I will surely prevail, I and My messengers!” Truly, God is Strong, Mighty!
versets.commentaire: God has inscribed (kataba) the victory of Himself and His messengers. Qushayrī sums it up in a single stroke: he who has only contriving (tadbīr), how could he resist the decree (taqdīr)? — the note says: contriving is for creatures, the decree is for the Real. Man bustles and calculates, but it is the divine decree that decides: to oppose one's contriving to God's decree is vain from the start.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / غ ل ب
mots.sens: God decreed: I will prevail, I and My messengers
mots.def: kataba: to decree; ghalaba: to prevail.

mots.racine: ق و ي / ع ز ز
mots.sens: God is Strong, Mighty

versets.ar: لَّا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ يُوَادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَوْ كَانُوا آبَاءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ ۖ وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ حِزْبُ اللَّهِ ۚ أَلَا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: lā tajidu qawman yuʾminūna billāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri yuwāddūna man ḥādda llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-law kānū ābāʾahum aw abnāʾahum aw ikhwānahum aw ʿashīratahum ulāʾika kataba fī qulūbihimu l-īmāna wa-ayyadahum bi-rūḥin minhu wa-yudkhiluhum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā raḍiya llāhu ʿanhum wa-raḍū ʿanhu ulāʾika ḥizbu llāhi alā inna ḥizba llāhi humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: You will not find a people who believe in God and the Last Day showing affection to those who oppose God and His Messenger, even if they are their fathers, sons, brothers, or kin. Upon their hearts God has inscribed faith and strengthened them with a Spirit from Him. He will admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. Those are the party of God. Indeed, the party of God are the successful!
versets.commentaire: The summit of the sūra. Qushayrī: whoever leans toward one dissolute in his religion, or flatters an innovator in his pact, God tears the light of tawḥīd from his heart. But to the faithful: God created faith in the hearts of His saints and made it firm there; He made their hearts a cloth embroidered with His Name — and what honour, a garment whose brocade, for the secrets of a people, is the Name “Allāh”! Such are the ḥizb Allāh, the party of God, pleased and well-pleasing (raḍiya llāhu ʿanhum wa-raḍū ʿanhu), the triumphant (al-mufliḥūn).

mots.ar: لَّا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ يُوَادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللَّهَ
mots.tr: lā tajidu qawman yuʾminūna billāhi yuwāddūna man ḥādda llāh
mots.racine: و ج د / و د د / ح د د
mots.sens: you find no believers loving who opposes God
mots.def: yuwāddu: to show affection; ḥādda: to oppose.

mots.ar: كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ
mots.tr: kataba fī qulūbihimu l-īmāna wa-ayyadahum bi-rūḥin minhu
mots.racine: ك ت ب / أ م ن / أ ي د / ر و ح
mots.sens: He inscribed faith in their hearts and strengthened them with a Spirit from Him
mots.def: kataba: to inscribe; ayyada: to strengthen; rūḥ: spirit.

mots.racine: ر ض و
mots.sens: God is pleased with them and they with Him

mots.racine: ح ز ب / ف ل ح
mots.sens: they are the party of God, the successful
mots.def: ḥizb Allāh: the party of God; mufliḥ: who succeeds.
